title: Limonium endlichianum
intro: Limonium endlichianum, also known as sea lavender, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and silvery-gray leaves. The health benefits of sea lavender include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing skin irritations, reducing stress and anxiety, and protecting against oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of sea lavender include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with sea lavender include teas, essential oils, and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
